rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF AIJNOS NOCOS MCFARLAND
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner paid $12,750 in tuition for the ward. He states that he relied upon his general authority as guardian of the estate under PC 2430(a)(2) to pay the tuition. He concedes that Local Rule 14.47(F), which references PC 2422, provides that Court approval must be obtained prior to using guardianship assets for the minor's education; however, he avers that PC 2422 does not apply here because he is not legally obligated to support the ward, and the ward's biological mother, who lives in the Philippines, has no funds to contribute to the ward's expenses. 2) Petitioner paid ~$2,000 ($200/month via direct payment and reimbursements) to a private gym for one-on-one training. 3) Petitioner paid an average of $1,400/month for rent and miscellaneous expenses despite only $1,300/month being allowed. Petitioner alleges that he thought that up to $1,400 per month could be paid taking into account the $100/month allowance. However, the accounting does not show $100 per month being distributed to the ward, and Petitioner only states that he provided $200 to the ward every time she obtained a higher than 3.5 GPA. 4) Petitioner purchased a ~$10k vehicle (2012 Jeep Patriot) for ward during this accounting period without court approval. Petitioner avers that PC 2451.5(b) allows guardian to purchase tangible personal property on behalf of the minor and that the purchase was necessary as public transportation was potentially hazardous during the pandemic and ward's stepmother does not drive. Petitioner states that the automobile is not listed as an asset of the estate because the car is titled under the ward's name. 5) Cash verification for US Bank 4578 was in the form of a printed "Transaction History", (which does not show how account is titled), which is insufficient verification. 6) Per bank statements filed, title to the two BofA accounts are in ITF (presumably "in trust for") and as a CUTMA, rather than in the name of the guardianship. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 10th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DONALD J. REBELLO LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. All residual beneficiaries named in the trust predeceased the settlor. The governing trust instrument did not provide for any contingent beneficiaries or other dispositions in the event of the named beneficiaries' failure to survive the settlor. Probate Code section 21111(a)(3) provides that: "If the transferring instrument does not provide for an alternative disposition and does not provide for the transfer of a residue, or if the transfer itself is a residuary gift, the property is transferred to the decedent's estate." The petition does not identify any property of the trust, other than anticipated distribution from the settlor's probate estate, as the trust is the beneficiary under the settlor's pour-over will. The petition alleges that outright distribution will be made to the settlor's intestate heirs from the trust by a successor trustee. The petition requests the petitioner's appointment as successor trustee, as the governing trust instrument did not name any successor trustees who remain alive. The petition requests a waiver of a successor trustee's bond. Probate Code section 15602(a)(3) provides in relevant part that: "(a) A trustee is not required to give a bond to secure performance of the trustee's duties, unless any of the following circumstances occurs: (3) An individual who is not named as a trustee in the trust instrument is appointed as a trustee by the court." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEONARD RUDOLF NELSON 2011 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Appointing Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition requests the petitioner's appointment as successor trustee, as the governing trust instrument did not name any successor trustees other than Bank of the West that has declined to serve. The petition requests a waiver of a successor trustee's bond. Probate Code section 15602(a)(3) provides in relevant part that: "(a) A trustee is not required to give a bond to secure performance of the trustee's duties, unless any of the following circumstances occurs: (3) An individual who is not named as a trustee in the trust instrument is appointed as a trustee by the court." Section 15602(b) provides in relevant part that: "The court may not excuse the requirement of a bond except under compelling circumstances. For purposes of this section, a request by all the adult beneficiaries of a trust that bond be waived is deemed to constitute a compelling circumstance." The San Francisco SPCA, as the sole trust beneficiary, consented to the petitioner's appointment and waived a bond, although he SPCA is not an adult beneficiary within the meaning of section 15602(b). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse.
